description: Vector (h): JCS canonicalization examples — NFC normalization, UTF-16 code-unit key order, integer representation, verbatim strings. Each case gives input(s), the expected canonical byte string, and its SHA-256.

cases.name: nfc-composed-vs-decomposed
cases.description: Composed U+00E9 and decomposed U+0065+U+0301 MUST canonicalize to identical bytes (4.1 NFC rule — beyond stock RFC 8785).

cases.name: key-order-utf16-code-units
cases.description: Member names sort by UTF-16 code unit: uppercase before lowercase; NOT locale/case-insensitive order.

cases.name: integer-representation
cases.description: Money and counts are integer JSON numbers: no decimals, no exponents, no strings (3.5/4.1).

cases.name: strings-verbatim
cases.description: Strings (timestamps included) are hashed verbatim — no timezone or precision rewriting.

cases.name: nested-sorting
cases.description: Sorting applies at every nesting level; arrays preserve order.
